item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images that were used in this article are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the first image comes from here and the second image comes from here, both with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the images have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This thing can do one hell of a burnout :) +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting idea, but this feels to me like one of those SCPs that changes a law of nature in isolation, and doesn't consider the wide-reaching implications.
if there is ever a rewrite, those holes could be covered with implied experiments, redacted results, and an upgrade to keter…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can see at least two potential infinite loops as well as a perpetuum mobile. On its own it is not complicated, but someone is going to try to experiment with it and break something (possibly including the sound barrier). Hell, you can build a space elevator with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do have to confess that my mental image for how this appears is similar to what you'd see in a classic cartoon, although much rougher.

Technically you could use it for excavation, but it would get pretty dangerous. The further down you go the more likely something heavy is going to fall on a strip of ground you're dragging…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the cartoonish effect. Upvoted.

Say, could you use this thing for excavation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: sweet ride, where can I get one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really neat. I was thinking of just this sort of effect recently but couldn't come up with a thing to attach it to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I may just be a sucker for "Safe objects with a whimsical property as long as it's not happening directly to you", but I like this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. I'll see if I can adjust the wording to make that more clear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good point. I didn't want to bloat the article too much, but I can probably add one more paragraph with some of those answers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Darn you mangling the coefficient of friction! +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-1187 in action: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nSj_MVUsBzg&feature=player_detailpage#t=50s

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No crashing, IE.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why say explicitly whether it's destructible? I don't see any mention of it being indestructible, so I assume it isn't, so there's no more reason for the article to say "it is not indestructible" than for it to say "it is not an infohazard".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can get on board with this ^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It doesn't do anything for me personally, so no upvote, but I get why other people might find it nifty, so no downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is actually pretty neat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really really enjoy the concept, for that alone I upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. I think that any time you designate an "incident", it should be given an appropriate designation, ex. Incident 1187-a

2. There are a lot of unanswered, but likely answerable questions in this.
Physically: Is it destructible? Are all components factory spec for the make and model? Are the components made of standard materials? Does it use fuel at the expected rate? Does the effect end if the unit runs out of fuel?

Operationally: Is the force generated by the wheels effectively unlimited (that is, does the ground move at 40km/h regardless of the mass being moved)? Does the anomalous effect occur on inclined ground? Does it occur if SCP-1187 is propped sideways against a wall (thereby moving the wall)? If it's placed on an inclined plane, will it roll down the hill, or will the hill roll up? Does the answer depend on whether the unit is running or not?

I wouldn't want you to include answers to ALL of the above questions, but answering a couple of them might add some meat and SCIENCE to the article ^_^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see now. So after that 250m gets lobbed behind, does it fall and begin dragging the next layer?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is just boring to me, I couldn't get into it really. There wouldn't be any need for further testing either it seems, because it is known what the thing does. Just meh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No. As long as it can get purchase it will literally drag the ground in front of the vehicle underneath it and pile it up behind it, and it effects a full 3m wide strip of ground, not just the area touched by the wheels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it works on the same principle as an ATV on very soft sand or mud? The surface around the tyres would get swept away, but as soon as the ATV is lowered to its base it would just sit there as if bogged while the tyres spin in place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any suggestions for either tone or parts you'd like to see elaborated?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a good idea, but there are a couple tone issues, and it feels rushed and hollow. I think I'm gonna -1 it, but change to +1 when it's reworked a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An attempt at taking a simple effect and making a Safe SCP from it. Comments and critiques always welcome.
